subject: Re: .tiff extention and/or symbolic link in Windows
body:
Hi Koji,

I've now included *.tiff in the upcoming 1.0.7W. Beta should be out 
tomorrow.

You can also create symbolic links on Windows, and it looks like Warp will 
handle them just like normal files. You can create them using 'mklink' from 
the command line. Make sure to use the /H option, as soft symlinks won't be 
discovered by Warp.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, April 9, 2019 at 4:53:53 AM UTC+2, yonelab.tem@gmail.com wrote:
>
>
> Hi Dimitry,
>
> We use JADAS for automatic data collection. This program produces movie 
> stacks with the *.tiff extension. Warp cannot treat this, and we rename it 
> to *.tif, but like to use *.tiff files directly. We'd appreciate it if you 
> could consider this option in the next release. Or I wonder if there is a 
> way to create symbolic links in Windows 10?
>
> Thank you in advance.
> Best regards,
>
> Koji
>
